title: sida subcuneata
intro: Sida subcuneata, also known as the wedge-leaf sida, is a type of flowering plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sida subcuneata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions. Sida subcuneata can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and salves for topical application.
